races.race_name: Connolly's RED MILLS Irish EBF Auction Maiden Hurdle (IRE Incentive)

horses.name: CHAMPAGNE JURY 27
horses.owner: Dochas & Mrs J P Duffy Partnership
horses.commentary: Bumper winner in the mud; gave Honesty Policy a contest when runner-up on hurdles debut at Leopardstown in March; good comeback third at Down Royal, headed by runner-up Dunleer Crystal late on; claims.

horses.name: IFALLGOESWELL 4
horses.commentary: Placed in three bumpers; last two efforts over hurdles promising, runner-up in Limerick novice (2m3f, soft to heavy) last month and was 2l clear approaching the last when filling the same spot at Thurles (2m4f) on Sunday; drop in trip should be fine.

horses.name: EAGLE'S QUEST 27
horses.owner: Jackie Cullen & A D Mullins & Ms Margaret Mullins

horses.name: LUNAR MAJESTY 25
horses.commentary: Order Of St George mare was 42,000euros 3yo but made no impression in a strong 20.5f maiden hurdle at Leopardstown on debut last month.

horses.name: ALL NIGHT REVIVAL 62
horses.commentary: Runner-up on sole point-to-point start for Nigel Slevin; beaten a neck in an average-looking bumper for same trainer at Fairyhouse (heavy) in November; has since joined Philip Rothwell; place chance.

horses.name: DUNLEER CRYSTAL 27
horses.commentary: Disappointed first two starts, including on heavy; major improvement on latest at Down Royal when runner-up (2m1f, yielding), overcoming bad mistake at the third, pipping Champagne Jury late on; big claims if he handles the ground.

horses.name: THEWAYBACKHOME 14
horses.commentary: Runner-up in one of his four point-to-point starts; never a factor on rules debut at Clonmel; best watched.

races.betting_forecast: 2/1 Dunleer Crystal, 5/2 Champagne Jury, 9/2 Ifallgoeswell, 11/2 Fabhcun, 9/1 All Night Revival, 12/1 Eagle's Quest, 40/1 Colin's
races.spotlight_verdict: Champagne Jury could reverse the form with Dunleer Crystal on much deeper ground but IFALLGOESWELL is turned out quickly after being nabbed late on at Thurles on Sunday and should be difficult to reel in down in trip. [Adrian Wall]

horses.name: PORTNACOO 56
horses.commentary: Four-time hurdles winner; not solid on his form this term, latterly for new yard; unseated rider at Taunton over fences on latest; best form is on better ground than this.

horses.name: DIXIE MAFIA 33
horses.commentary: Record of 2-8 in point-to-points; good second at Aintree on handicap hurdle debut (3m, soft); kept on late when 6l fifth of nine at Ascot two weeks later; British trainer's first runner at the track; watch the betting.

horses.name: WELL DEL 24
horses.commentary: Won three-runner beginners' chase for Liam O'Brien at Limerick last season; poor form thereafter; second at Fairyhouse on stable debut before creditable third at Leopardstown (both over fences); yet to convince in this sphere.

horses.name: KINGKONG CIERGUES 62
horses.commentary: Point-to-point winner was rewarded for his consistency over hurdles when scoring decisively at Fairyhouse in November (24.5f, heavy); the second and third have won since; 8lb higher but has to be in the shake-up again.

races.betting_forecast: 11/4 Kingkong Ciergues, 6/1 Joyau De Thaix, 7/1 The Lovely Man, 15/2 Dixie Mafia, 8/1 Well Del, 9/1 West Cork Wildway, 12/1
races.spotlight_verdict: The form of KINGKONG CIERGUES's decisive Fairyhouse win has worked out really well and he's fancied to defy an 8lb rise. Mudlover and the generally consistent Positive Thinker rates as an each-way alternative. British raider Dixie Mafia is worth a market check. [Adrian Wall]

horses.name: MOZZIES SISTER 60
horses.owner: For The Love Of It Syndicate
horses.commentary: Consistent four-time winner (two bumpers, two hurdle races); only once out of the first three and that when fourth in a Grade 3 event; kept good company last season; decent form again this term but not in the same league as some of these rivals, including stablemate Rocky's Diamond.

races.betting_forecast: 2/1 Staffordshire Knot, 3/1 Rocky's Diamond, 7/2 Gerri Colombe, Home By The Lee, 14/1 Mozzies Sister, 16/1 Franciscan Rock,
races.spotlight_verdict: Having looked highly progressive when winning this race last year, ROCKY'S DIAMOND has suffered reverses this term. If back on song, he could be hard to beat in a race in which Gordon Elliott sets a puzzle by saddling the in-form Staffordshire Knot and Gerri Colombe, who is bidding to get his career back on track. The veteran Home By The Lee is also a strong contender. [Alan Sweetman]

horses.name: JUST FOR ONE 24
horses.commentary: Couple of okay runs in maidens before tailed off over 2m4f on handicap debut at Cork; career-best when second of 15 in Limerick maiden last month (2m3f, soft to heavy); claims if he can build on that.

horses.name: COFFEYS FORGE 14
horses.commentary: Point-to-point winner; won beginners' chase for Gordon Elliott last May; 0-16 over hurdles but mistake at the last costly when runner-up at Clonmel a fortnight ago (2m4f, heavy); this trip is on the sharp side.

horses.name: WESTOFTIGNES 67
horses.commentary: Mild promise in maidens at 20.5f-2m6f; never involved on handicap debut at Punchestown but eyecatching headway to finish fourth of 24 at Navan (2m, heavy); could go well from 1lb lower.

horses.name: HARTY CUP 24
horses.commentary: Placed in a point-to-point last March; well beaten in four maiden hurdles, including behind Just For One at Limerick on latest, but you'd be loath to rule him out for these connections on his handicap debut; watch the betting.

horses.name: SCARLET PURSE 24
horses.commentary: Yet to show winning potential; tailed off on return from a break at Clonmel; beaten out of sight at Limerick last month; may need more time.

horses.name: GLENS SENSATION 7
horses.commentary: Point-to-point winner; yet to show signs of winning potential in eight starts over hurdles; difficult to fancy at present.

horses.name: KOOLIO KATE 40
horses.commentary: Minor encouragement in fourth place in Limerick maiden hurdle in November but failed to build on that on handicap debut at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) last month.

horses.name: BRUNO FROM MARS 22
horses.commentary: Maiden is 0-7 over hurdles; failed to complete first two handicaps; best effort yet when fourth of 18 at Punchestown (16.5f, yielding) on New Year's Eve but will face more testing conditions here.

horses.name: SIDIRIYA 14
horses.commentary: Second runner for yard; Flat winner; maiden hurdler; fair effort at Clonmel on penultimate; always in rear there two weeks ago despite going off 9-2; plenty to prove.

horses.name: POETS OATH 27
horses.commentary: Second runner for yard; big odds when tailed off in all four maiden starts but stable known for improving these types so a close market watch is advised on handicap debut.

horses.name: CHESTERBAYLAD 27
horses.commentary: Now 0-11 over hurdles and yet to show signs of winning potential; no improvement for softer conditions last couple of outings; hard to make a case for.

horses.name: RAJJNATIN 40
horses.commentary: Punchestown sixth in November was a step in the right direction; improvement not sustained at Clonmel; finished last of the finishers at Fairyhouse on latest.

horses.name: LEJOLICOEUR DU MOU 2
horses.commentary: Second reserve; no sign of worthwhile form in maidens; tailed off on handicap debut at Fairyhouse; never a threat when sixth at Down Royal on Tuesday.

races.betting_forecast: 5/1 Coffeys Forge, King Bob, 15/2 Westoftignes, 8/1 Just For One, 11/1 Bruno From Mars, 12/1 Uncle Gerhard, 14/1 Harty Cup,
races.spotlight_verdict: A real puzzle but a chance is taken on MICKEY HULIE who wasn't without support on his handicap debut and the application of a tongue-tie might help. King Bob is a big player if he handles the ground, while a market watch is advised for the Robert Tyner-trained pair Harty Cup and Poets Oath. [Adrian Wall]

horses.name: HAVEIGOTNEWSFORYOU 615
horses.commentary: Absent since a maiden hurdle win over 2m3f at Kilbeggan in May 2024; runner-up Gaucher turned out be far superior to a typical Kilbeggan winner; however, he faces a huge task after such a long layoff.

horses.name: SOLDIER IN MILAN 25
horses.commentary: Won a point-to-point by a wide margin in March 2024; ran out a comfortable winner of a Punchestown bumper 13 months later (2m3f, yielding); clinically abnormal when pulled up at Leopardstown last month on chasing debut; remains promising.

horses.name: RAFFLES DOLCE VITA 53
horses.commentary: Winning hurdler in France at three; struggled in two Listed hurdle outings last March; faded into sixth at Naas on Irish/chasing debut; out of his depth in Grade 1 Drinmore on latest.

races.betting_forecast: 2/1 Funiculi Funicula, 100/30 King Alexander, 11/2 Soldier In Milan, 6/1 Butch Cassidy, 7/1 The Wallpark, 8/1 C'Est Ta Chance,
races.spotlight_verdict: Willie Mullins has won this in seven of the last ten seasons. Paul Townend stays loyal to FUNICULI FUNICULA (nap), so there is a good case for believing he can reverse Naas form with King Alexander. There was an excuse for Soldier In Milan on his chasing debut. It is harder to forgive The Wallpark for a series of lapses. [Alan Sweetman]

races.race_name: Goffs Thyestes Handicap Chase (Grade 3)                                                                                    €100,000 Total Race

horses.name: HIGH CLASS HERO 61
horses.owner: Sullivan Group Limited & P Crowley & Neill Hughes
horses.commentary: Four-time hurdles winner; won a beginners' chase at Punchestown last February; beaten at short odds in a Thurles Grade 3 next time; better effort raised to 3m4f when a fine fourth in the bet365 Gold Cup; good run over an extended 2m5f in a handicap at Punchestown two months ago; return to longer trip should suit; one of the stable's prime candidates

horses.name: ANSWER TO KAYF 67
horses.commentary: Got off the mark over fences at the third attempt off 130 at Naas last January (2m4f, soft); failed to make an impact in the Jack Richards at Cheltenham; faded from two out in Punchestown festival handicap over a near-4m trip; produced in fine shape to win the Troytown on reappearance, beating Yeah Man and earning a 9lb hike in the ratings; can remain competitive.

horses.name: JORDANS 25
horses.owner: Cheeky Pups Syndicate                                                                 J P O'Brien
horses.commentary: Twice second in Grade 1 novice events last season, beaten by Impaire Et Passe at Limerick and by Caldwell Potter at Aintree; in between, finished fifth (third-best of the Irish) in a Cheltenham Festival handicap; runs this term have been in handicap hurdles off a much lower mark; caught the eye with a staying-on fifth at Leopardstown; can be rated a lively each-way option.

horses.name: SHANBALLY KID 26
horses.commentary: Finished third in the 2024 Coral Cup at Cheltenham; failed to make any impact in his first five chase starts; step up in trip brought out major improvement when a 15l winner of Punchestown festival handicap over just short of 4m; hit with a 14lb rise; should be sharper than when reappearing in the Paddy Power.

horses.name: CONYERS HILL 25
horses.commentary: Two good runs at Cheltenham this season, including fourth in the Paddy Power in November, and a creditable second at Limerick on his seasonal debut the previous month; in the lead but beginning to look vulnerable when falling two out in the Tim Duggan at Limerick on latest.

horses.name: BETTER TIMES AHEAD 53
horses.commentary: Runner-up in two handicap chases at around 3m in the spring of 2024; below that level over fences in three chase starts last season; exploited a 14lb lower hurdle mark to win over 3m at Fairyhouse on seasonal debut; made sustained headway from well off the pace to touch off Will Do in the Porterstown at the same venue; 9lb higher, still on a handy weight.

horses.name: LONESOME BOATMAN 53
horses.commentary: First reserve; four-time point winner; put in a prominent showing before fading into fourth in a 3m7f handicap at last year's Punchestown festival; made a pleasing start to the season when fourth over 3m at Listowel; relished the extra half-mile when making all to land the 3m4f Cork National; solid run off 9lb higher when third in the Porterstown.

horses.name: UNCLE PAT 25
horses.owner: Patrick Corbett & Michael Corbett & J J Corbett &
horses.commentary: Second reserve; bumper and maiden hurdle winner; fair form in first three beginners' chases, including fourth in useful 2m6f contest at Listowel; tailed off on testing ground at Navan early last month; much better when chasing home an easy winner on handicap debut at Limerick.

horses.name: BALLYGOREY RUBY 13
horses.commentary: Huge price when last of nine at Naas 13 days ago; can be left out of calculations.

races.betting_forecast: 5/4 Our Trigger, 2/1 Huntin Boots, 3/1 Hygge, 8/1 Infinity Succes, 100/1 Ballygorey Ruby.
races.spotlight_verdict: A brother to Gaelic Warrior, OUR TRIGGER gets the vote to see off a two-pronged challenge from the Gordon Elliott team. Newcomer Huntin Boots may pose a bigger threat than Hygge, whose form in "academy" hurdle races implies limitations. [Alan Sweetman]
